replication_study.hypothesis: In a one-week longitudinal SEM, baseline (T1) perceived realistic COVID-19 threat positively predicts negative affect at follow-up (T2), controlling for concurrent symbolic threat and allowing for covariance between predictors and among multiple T2 outcomes.

data_plan.wave_or_subset: Two-wave design approximately one week apart; analysis uses participants with both T1 and T2 responses identified via participant_id and created timestamps.
data_plan.sample_size: Approx. 243 participants with matched T1–T2 (per Analysis_updated.Rmd notes); starting raw rows n=633 (two waves combined). Final N determined at runtime post-exclusions.
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Individual participant (T2 outcomes regressed on T1 threats).
data_plan.access_details: Data provided in replication_data folder: Kachanoff_Survey_deidentify.csv; no external request needed.

qualification.explanation: The dataset mirrors the original study’s constructs, timing, and measures (realistic and symbolic threats; PANAS negative affect; ~1-week interval) but is a different sample collected later via Prolific. This allows testing whether the focal association generalizes across an independent participant pool and time period while preserving high construct fidelity.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Measures and design match: the Integrated COVID-19 Threat Scale with 5 realistic (covid_real1–5) and 5 symbolic (covid_symbolic1–5) items, and PANAS negative affect (negative1–negative10) are present (replication_data/Kachanoff_Survey_deidentify.csv; Analysis_updated.Rmd). The original paper used the same constructs and a ~1-week longitudinal design with threats at T1 predicting T2 outcomes (original_paper.pdf summary; post_registration.json method/results). The focal claim concerns T1 realistic threat predicting T2 negative affect (initial_details.txt).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Sampling frame/time differ: original used MTurk in March 2020 (post_registration.json; original_paper.pdf), whereas this dataset is Prolific with a 2021-01-18 date threshold and session-based exclusions (Analysis_updated.Rmd). The code deidentifies PROLIFIC_PID to participant_id, confirming a distinct sample source. Therefore, this is a new sample while maintaining the same operational definitions.

data_plan.notes: Time fields use America/New_York and may be altered by spreadsheet software; code accounts for this (Analysis_updated.Rmd). Composite scores handle missing data via NA-tolerant sums/means and 80% completeness for IES scales. SEM uses a wide dataset by merging T1/T2 rows per participant. Attention check-based exclusions are applied. Variable naming: covid_real*, covid_symbolic*, negative*, positive*, swls*, intrusion*, avoid*, handwashing, social*, sds*, behave_*. Data import paths adjusted to /app/data.

steps: Apply data cleaning and exclusions as in Analysis_updated.Rmd (session de-duplication, missing PROLIFIC assignment fixes, attention checks, 80% completeness rules).
Create composite scores: Realistic (mean of covid_real1–5); Symbolic (mean of covid_symbolic1–5); Negative affect (sum of negative1–negative10); and other outcomes as in code.
Order by created, split into T1 vs T2 using duplicated(participant_id), then merge to wide (DFwide) with .x=T1 and .y=T2 suffixes.
Fit the SEM specifying Negative.y ~ Realistic.x + Symbolic.x (plus the other outcomes as in code), with Realistic.x ~~ Symbolic.x and residual covariances among T2 DVs.
Extract parameter estimates and fit indices; record coefficient and p-value for Realistic.x -> Negative.y.
planned_method.models: Structural Equation Model (lavaan) with multiple T2 outcomes regressed on T1 threats; predictors covary; residual covariances among T2 outcomes.
planned_method.outcome_variable: T2 negative affect (Negative.y: sum of negative1–negative10 at T2).
planned_method.independent_variables: T1 perceived realistic threat (Realistic.x: mean of covid_real1–5) and T1 perceived symbolic threat (Symbolic.x: mean of covid_symbolic1–5).
planned_method.control_variables: Symbolic threat included as covariate; predictors covary (Realistic.x ~~ Symbolic.x). Residual covariances among T2 outcomes are specified per the original model.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Unstandardized regression coefficient for Realistic.x predicting Negative.y in the SEM; optionally report standardized estimate.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Wald z-test (reported by lavaan) for the coefficient; two-tailed.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Scale-level: sums/means computed with na.rm=TRUE; IES composites require >=80% item completion. SEM: default listwise deletion in lavaan on DFwide.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Focal test is Realistic.x -> Negative.y. No multiple testing correction for other outcomes; report them as exploratory/secondary.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Primary success if Realistic.x -> Negative.y is positive and statistically significant at alpha=0.05 (two-tailed), with magnitude reasonably close to the original unstandardized effect (b≈4.02) given scale/measurement comparability.

files.Analysis_updated.Rmd: Main replication analysis. Imports /app/data/Kachanoff_Survey_deidentify.csv, performs cleaning, creates composites (Realistic, Symbolic, Negative, etc.), constructs T1–T2 wide data, and fits the SEM including Negative.y ~ Realistic.x + Symbolic.x. Contains attention-check rules, de-duplication, and timestamp-based filtering.
files.Analysis.Rmd: Earlier version using an example CSV; includes similar composite creation and SEM. Now points to /app/data/Kachanoff_Survey_example.csv after path fix. Provided mainly for reference.
files.Kachanoff_Survey_deidentify.csv: De-identified two-wave dataset with item-level responses (covid_real*, covid_symbolic*, negative*, positive*, swls*, intrusion*, avoid*, handwashing, social*, sds*, behave_*), timestamps (created), session, and hashed participant_id.
files.Kachanoff_Survey_example.csv: Illustrative/example dataset for the earlier Analysis.Rmd.
files.run.sh: Shell wrapper to render the RMarkdown: runs rmarkdown::render('replication_data/Analysis_updated.Rmd') and writes output to /app/artifacts.

codebase.notes: All data reads updated to absolute path /app/data per run policy. No files are written by default; analysis outputs are printed to console/knit output. Required R packages: lavaan, rio, openssl, knitr, rmarkdown. Entry point: bash run.sh.

instructions: Run R and render the Analysis_updated.Rmd, or source its chunks in order.
Confirm cleaning/exclusions executed (attention checks, session exclusions, timestamp filtering).
Verify DFwide is created and SEM runs without errors.
Extract parameter estimates for Realistic.x -> Negative.y with p-values and CIs (parameterestimates(fit1)).
Optionally save parameter estimates to a CSV under /app/data for reporting.
comparison_metrics: Unstandardized coefficient magnitude versus original (target b≈4.02; note that exact comparability depends on scoring and sample).
Confidence interval overlap with original (if reported) or plausible magnitude range.
Robustness across model specifications (e.g., standardized estimates).
